div1.entryFree:
Chariot, 1) a carriage of state, especially a triumphal car: their mistress (viz Venus) in her light c. quickly is conveyed, Ven. 1192. in a captive c. bring him our prisoner, H5 III, 5, 54. my sword, my c. and my prisoners, Tit. I, 249. horse and --s let us have, II, 2, 18. her (Queen Mab's) c. is an empty hazel-nut, Rom. I, 4, 67. and when you saw his c. but appear, Caes. I, 1, 48. shall set thee on triumphant --s, Ant. III, 1, 10. follow his c. IV, 12, 35. seated in a c. Per. II, 4, 7.
2) a car formerly used in war: it fits us therefore ripely our --s and our horsemen be in readiness, Cymb. III, 5, 23.
